user.title: Connect to TimescaleDB
user.description:
Enter the PostgreSQL connection URL for your TimescaleDB instance.

Format: `postgresql://user:password@host:5432/dbname`

Additional options (SSL, filtering, chunk intervals) are available after setup via **Configure**.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the database. Check the URL and make sure PostgreSQL is reachable.

abort.already_configured: Scribe is already configured. Use **Configure** to change settings.
abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one Scribe instance is allowed.

data_description.record_states: Write every state change to TimescaleDB
data_description.record_events: Write Home Assistant events (automations, scripts…) to TimescaleDB
data_description.batch_size: Number of rows accumulated before a flush is triggered (default 500)
data_description.max_queue_size: Maximum pending writes before oldest are dropped (default 10000)
data_description.buffer_on_failure: Keep events in memory and retry when the database becomes available again
data_description.include_domains: Only record entities from these domains (e.g. sensor, light). Leave empty to include all.
data_description.exclude_domains: Never record entities from these domains
data_description.include_entities: Only record these specific entities. Leave empty to include all.
data_description.include_entity_globs: Only record entities matching these patterns (e.g. sensor.temp_*)
data_description.exclude_entity_globs: Never record entities matching these patterns
data_description.exclude_attributes: Drop these attribute keys before writing (saves space)

error.must_record_something: At least one of 'Record state changes' or 'Record events' must be enabled.
